root_text.an6.24:1.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu himavantaṁ pabbatarājaṁ padāleyya, ko pana vādo chavāya avijjāya.
root_text.an6.24:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu samādhissa samāpattikusalo hoti, samādhissa ṭhitikusalo hoti, samādhissa vuṭṭhānakusalo hoti, samādhissa kallitakusalo hoti, samādhissa gocarakusalo hoti, samādhissa abhinīhārakusalo hoti.
root_text.an6.24:1.4: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu himavantaṁ pabbatarājaṁ padāleyya, ko pana vādo chavāya avijjāyā”ti.

translation_text.an6.24:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant who has six qualities could shatter Himalaya, the king of mountains, let alone this wretched ignorance!
translation_text.an6.24:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is skilled in entering immersion, skilled in remaining in immersion, skilled in emerging from immersion, skilled in positivity for immersion, skilled in the territory of immersion, and skilled in projecting the mind purified by immersion.
translation_text.an6.24:1.4: A mendicant who possesses these six qualities could shatter Himalaya, the king of mountains, let alone this wretched ignorance!”

variant_text.an6.24:1.3: kallitakusalo → kallatākusalo (bj, sya-all, km, mr); kallitākusalo (pts1ed)
